title: brain index rebuild — --json success output
description: Full regeneration of the retrieval index from Markdown. Re-chunks and re-embeds every projected note, writes fresh generations, and activates them via the SQLite CAS (retrieval-index-contract.md §2/§3), reconstructing the LanceDB index even when it was deleted wholesale (the directory is disposable derived state). Intrinsically idempotent and projection-only: each executed run appends exactly one run.projection git-ref audit event and never touches the ledger tables, the vault, or canonical git refs. This is the step registered into the post-restore hook, so db restore ends with a consistent rebuilt index (completes R1-F1's restore contract).

chunksWritten.description: Total SearchChunk rows written for the new generations.

generationsRetired.description: Prior-generation chunk sets retired after activation.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Full index regeneration from Markdown (§3); reconstructs a deleted LanceDB index; projection-only, idempotent; registered into the post-restore hook.
sideEffects: re-chunks, re-embeds, and activates every projected note; retires prior generations
emits exactly one run.projection git-ref audit event (executed projection-only command)
prohibitedEffects: never touches ledger tables or db_schema_migrations
a crash mid-rebuild must leave the prior active generation readable and converge on rerun

errorCodes.when: the vault is missing/unreadable so chunks cannot be re-derived
the SQLite store file is missing or unopenable
the exclusive vault-maintenance lock is held by a live process

a batch embed hit a provider-retryable ProviderError (rate_limit/quota/timeout/transport); envelope carries retryable:true and retryAfterMs when the provider supplied timing — the caller may re-run rebuild to converge
a batch embed failed with a non-retryable ProviderError (authentication/model_incompatible/embedding-dimension mismatch)
the rebuild could not complete and was rolled back
